commit.message:
Redesign T1 finding layouts with consistent content flow

All 6 findings now follow: Title → Hook (subtitle in quotes) →
Body paragraph → Data point (stat) → Bullets.

3 visually distinct layouts cycling across F01-F06:
- Layout A (F01, F04): Narrow left rail with stat/label, wide right column for narrative
- Layout B (F02, F05): Full-width stack — title+hook side by side, body, then stat+bullets row
- Layout C (F03, F06): Column stack — title, hook, body, then stat+bullets at bottom

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add WASM directive and instance caching for shared .wasm modules

Multiple .js user functions can now share a single .wasm module via
a `// wasm: finufft` directive. Instances are cached so they share
linear memory and malloc state.

commit.message:
confluence-mdx: sidecar mapping을 텍스트 비교에서 타입 기반 순차 정렬로 교체합니다 (#901)

## 배경

`generate_sidecar_mapping()`이 XHTML 블록과 MDX 블록을 매칭할 때 텍스트 유사도 비교를
사용했습니다.
이로 인해 두 가지 문제가 있었습니다:

1. **매칭 정확도 낮음** — emoticon, lost_info 등 텍스트 차이로 오매칭 발생
2. **폴백 체인 누적** — 매칭 실패를 보완하기 위해 `_resolve_child_mapping()` 4단계 폴백과
`_find_containing_mapping()` 텍스트 포함 검색이 추가됨

## 변경 내용

### 1. `sidecar.py`: 타입 기반 two-pointer 매칭 (mapping.yaml v3)

`_TYPE_COMPAT` 테이블로 XHTML/MDX 블록 타입 호환성을 선언하고 두 포인터를 순차 진행하며 매칭합니다.
텍스트 비교를 일절 사용하지 않습니다.

sidecar 엔트리에 다음 필드를 추가합니다:

- `xhtml_type`: XHTML 블록의 타입
- `mdx_line_start` / `mdx_line_end`: 대응 MDX 블록의 줄 번호
- `children`: compound 블록(callout 등)의 자식 정렬 정보

타입 불일치 시 XHTML 블록에 MDX 대응 없음으로 처리합니다 (ac:image, toc 등 forward converter가
MDX로 출력하지 않는 블록).

### 2. `list_patcher.py`: 항목 변경 시 항상 전체 리스트 재생성

`_resolve_child_mapping()` (4단계 텍스트 폴백)을 삭제합니다.
변경된 항목이 하나라도 있으면 전체 리스트 inner XHTML을 재생성합니다.

### 3. `patch_builder.py`: 텍스트 포함 검색 제거

`_find_containing_mapping()` (텍스트 포함 검색)과 `_strip_block_markers()`를
삭제합니다.
`_resolve_mapping_for_change()`에서 다음 규칙을 명시적으로 선언합니다:

- callout → 항상 `containing` 전략
- children 있는 mapping → `containing` 또는 `list` 전략
- sidecar 조회 실패 → `skip`

## 삭제된 코드

| 함수 | 줄 수 | 이유 |
|------|-------|------|
| `_resolve_child_mapping()` | ~70줄 | 타입 기반 sidecar로 불필요 |
| `_find_containing_mapping()` | ~25줄 | sidecar O(1) 조회로 대체 |
| `_strip_block_markers()` | ~5줄 | `_find_containing_mapping` 삭제로 불필요 |

## Added/updated tests?

- [x] Yes — `test_reverse_sync_sidecar.py`: 타입 기반 매핑 단위 테스트
- [x] Yes — `test_reverse_sync_patch_builder.py`: 단순화된 전략 결정 테스트
- [x] Yes — `test_reverse_sync_cli.py`: v3 스키마 E2E 테스트
- [x] Yes — testcase expected 파일 갱신

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(gpu-terminal): add visibility fallback for session restore reliability

The 'loaded' handler's async IIFE had no error handling, so any failure
silently dropped all sessions. Add .catch() for logging and a new
ensureSessionsSent() safety net that resends sessions on visibility
change — the webview's addSession() skips duplicates, making this
a zero-cost fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(donaciones): arreglos formulario anotar donación

- Limitar ancho del formulario (max-width 820px centrado)
- Reducir inputs monetarios y de pago a col-sm-2/3
- Reemplazar 3 selects de fecha por datepicker nativo
- Exclusividad NIF/email en modales de búsqueda
- Añadir endpoint AJAX buscarDonantePorDocEmail al controlador
  (modales buscar socio/donante previo ahora funcionan)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(deploy): resolve broken @immorterm/* symlinks during extension deploy

bun workspace creates relative symlinks for local packages in
node_modules/@immorterm/. These break when copied outside the monorepo
to ~/.vscode/extensions/, causing require("@immorterm/menu-data") to
fail at runtime and the GPU terminal to not initialize.

The build script now auto-detects and replaces broken symlinks with
real copies during the deploy step.

schema, then auto-fills any missing required parameters with empty
string. This works around the n8n mcpTrigger v2 bug where ALL
workflowInputs.value keys are marked as required (GitHub #17981).

so ensureRecurringGoalsForToday() doesn't re-add them each render cycle.
Filter deleted goals out in renderGoals() so they stay invisible.

Add index store enrichment for compiler-resolved call edges, overrides, and USRs

Integrates swiftlang/indexstore-db to read the compiler's index store as a
post-processing step after AST-based indexing. This populates symbol USRs,
creates compiler-resolved call edges, override edges (method → superclass),
and implementsRequirement edges (concrete → protocol requirement). The
enrichment is optional and silently skipped if no index store is available.

commit.message:
fix(admin): permanent user deletion via SECURITY DEFINER RPC

Root cause: supabase.from('profiles').delete() was silently blocked by
RLS (no DELETE policy existed). Even if it had succeeded, deleting only
the profile row leaves the auth.users record intact, so the user could
log back in and the row would reappear on next data fetch.

Fix:
- Migration 025: create delete_user_account(uuid) RPC with SECURITY
  DEFINER — runs as DB owner, verifies caller is_admin(), prevents
  self-deletion, deletes from auth.users (which CASCADE-deletes the
  profile and all linked rows), raises if user not found.
- AdminDashboard: call supabase.rpc('delete_user_account') instead of
  direct table delete; log + surface RPC errors instead of silently
  treating RLS blocks as success.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix silent Resend errors and email content

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

add frame table support to read_img

Adds a `frames` parameter to `read_img` accepting a path to a CSV file
that explicitly maps each physical frame index to a (color, z) pair.
This enables loading images with ragged and non-square frame ordering
without relying solely on XML metadata inference.

Key features:
- `_read_frame_table`: new helper that parses the CSV and builds a
  (color, z_index) -> frame_index lookup dict
- Frame count validation: compared against XML `number_frames` when
  available, otherwise verified by attempting to reshape the raw dax data
- Intelligent axis inference: when only `colors` is specified, z_slices
  are inferred as the z-indices where all requested colors have frames;
  when only `z_slices` is specified, colors are inferred as those present
  at all requested z-planes; explicit invalid combinations still error
- Adds CLAUDE.md with project conventions and test instructions
- Adds frame table test fixtures and tests covering square tables,
  frame count mismatch, and ragged acquisition inference

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
